file_insights: Created offline_interceptor.dart (536 lines): Generic interceptor for any write operation with connectivity checking and queue management
Created mutation_interceptor.dart (668 lines): Dio-specific interceptor for GraphQL mutations with network error handling
Updated offline_sync.dart barrel to export both interceptors
Updated sync_operation.dart entity with operation type enum (CREATE, UPDATE, DELETE)
Created sync_queue_repository.dart interface with queue operation methods
Created sync_operation_model.dart with database entity mapping
Updated sync_queue_service.dart with queue management methods
patterns_discovered: Generic interceptor pattern with configurable behavior via InterceptorConfig
Mutation-specific interceptor pattern integrating with Dio request pipeline
Stream-based reactive programming for operation results
Optimistic UI update pattern with immediate local database callbacks
Batch operation support for performance optimization
Error recovery pattern with queue operations on network failures
Debug logging with emoji indicators for traceability
Comprehensive result tracking with InterceptorResult and MutationInterceptorResult
gotchas_discovered: Need proper connectivity checking before operation execution
Offline queuing requires optimistic UI responses for user feedback
GraphQL mutation metadata parsing requires careful error handling
Local database updates must be immediate but queued operations need proper tracking
Interceptor integration with existing Dio pipeline requires careful configuration
Batch operations need proper queue management and result aggregation
Memory leaks must be prevented with proper stream disposal

approach_outcome.approach_used: Implemented comprehensive operation interceptors following clean architecture patterns, creating both generic and mutation-specific interceptors with proper connectivity checking, queue management, and optimistic UI updates
approach_outcome.why_it_worked: Followed established project patterns from previous repository implementations, created modular interceptor design supporting both generic and GraphQL-specific use cases, implemented comprehensive acceptance criteria coverage, maintained consistency with existing codebase patterns, and provided extensive documentation

recommendations: Add operation retry logic for queued operations that fail during network recovery
Implement operation priority system for queued operations (critical vs normal operations)
Add interceptor configuration validation to prevent misconfiguration
Consider implementing interceptor metrics/analytics for monitoring operation success rates
Add support for operation cancellation if queued operations become stale
Implement operation conflict detection for queued operations
Add interceptor unit testing with mock connectivity and queue services
Consider implementing interceptor performance monitoring for operation timing
